canonical_definition: The quality of an idea being statistically infrequent or uncommon relative to a population of ideas.

relationships.evidence: “Creativity scoring broadly considers two complementary dimensions: the intrinsic qualities of ideas ... and their extrinsic statistical infrequency within a population (i.e., original ideas do not appear very often)”

relationships.paper: On the Same Wavelength? Evaluating Pragmatic Reasoning in Language Models across Broad Concepts
relationships.evidence: “For convergent validity, MUSESCORER’s normalized originality scores {Othresh_i} correlate strongly with Creativity Quotient (CQ) scores in socialmuse24. CQ is a flexibility measure that captures the diversity of semantic categories.”
